drivers.name: Meyer Sound Galaxy 816
drivers.author: CuePlot
drivers.description: Controls Meyer Sound Galileo GALAXY 816 processors over the ASCII control port. Includes snapshot recall, input/output mute and gain, and matrix gain.

help.overview: Controls Meyer Sound Galileo GALAXY processors through the documented ASCII control port on TCP 25003. This driver focuses on the operational commands most commonly needed in show control: snapshot recall, input/output mute and gain, and matrix crosspoint gain.
help.setup:
1. Connect the GALAXY to the same network as the CuePlot server.
2. In Compass or from the front panel, note the device IP address or mDNS hostname.
3. Add the device in CuePlot and enter the host. Default ASCII port is 25003.
4. If you want live state feedback, set the Primary Input / Output / Matrix channels below to the channels you want CuePlot to track.
5. Test with Query Entity Name or Query Active Snapshot before building automation.

compatible_models.notes: Uses the GALAXY ASCII/TCP control plane documented by Meyer Sound.

drivers.name: Digital Projection E-Vision Native
drivers.author: CuePlot
drivers.description: Native ASCII protocol driver for Digital Projection E-Vision Laser 7500/8500. Covers power, shutter, inputs, test patterns, lens, image, color, geometry, edge blend, 3D, laser, setup, network, PIP, information, and diagnostics.

help.overview: Native Digital Projection driver using the documented ASCII command protocol. Exposes power, shutter, inputs, test patterns, lens, image, color, geometry, edge blend, 3D, laser, scheduling, network, PIP, information, and diagnostics.
help.setup:
1. Put the projector on the same network as CuePlot.
2. Confirm the projector TCP control port is 7000.
3. Add the device with its IP address and port 7000.
4. Use Refresh / Query All Status to verify communications.
5. Build macros only after power, shutter, input, and lens commands respond correctly.

compatible_models.notes: Implements the native protocol documented in Digital Projection Protocol Guides Rev F (115-482F, October 2016).

drivers.name: PIXERA Timeline Controller
drivers.author: CuePlot
drivers.description: JSON-RPC timeline control driver for AV Stumpfl PIXERA media servers. Covers transport, cue recall, timeline time, speed factor, and opacity over JSON/TCP (dl).

help.overview: Controls a PIXERA timeline through the official JSON-RPC API in JSON/TCP (dl) mode. This version targets show operation: play, pause, stop, cue recall, transport mode, current time, speed, opacity, and cue inspection.
help.setup:
1. Put the PIXERA server on the same network as CuePlot.
2. Enable the PIXERA API port.
3. Set the API mode to JSON/TCP (dl).
4. Use port 1400 unless your PIXERA system is configured differently.
5. Set a preferred timeline name in the driver config if the project has multiple timelines.

compatible_models.notes: Implements the PIXERA JSON-RPC API documented at help.pixera.one/api-commands.
